content: Sigaar: Nice day for a ride, eh? The roads are clear, and the sun’s shining—perfect for traveling... or other outdoor activities, if you catch my meaning. Lydia: Hmph. Best keep your hands to yourself, carriage rat. My Thane’s axe would split your skull before you could blink. Sigaar: Easy now, housecarl. Just making polite conversation. Saw a group of bandits getting... with a pack of wolves near the pass yesterday. Damndest thing. Skyrim’s gone madder than a skeever in heat. Lydia: Disgusting. That’s what happens when you let filth like the Forsworn and Thalmor roam free. Bet they’d fuck a troll if it stood still long enough. Sigaar: Heh. Speaking of, you ever seen a giant take down a traveler? Brutal stuff. Then again, some folk pay extra for that kind of ride. Lydia: By Diabella’s tits, I swear—men like you are why this province’s crumbling. No honor, just perversion and weakness. Sigaar: Says the woman wearing a slave-queen’s brassiere into battle. traditional values

Sigaar: Aye, well, enjoy your stroll through Riften. Just don’t let the Thieves’ Guild pick your pockets—unless you’re into that. Lydia: I’ll gut any rat that tries. Come on, Thane—let’s get to the Bee and Barb before I lose my temper and decorate the street with this fool’s guts. Sigaar: Till next time, housecarl. Safe travels—or not. Up to you.
